root_text.an10.94:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā campāyaṁ viharati gaggarāya pokkharaṇiyā tīre.
root_text.an10.94:1.2: Atha kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati divā divassa campāya nikkhami bhagavantaṁ dassanāya.
root_text.an10.94:1.8: Yannūnāhaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyan”ti.
root_text.an10.94:2.1: Atha kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.an10.94:2.2: Tena kho pana samayena te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā saṅgamma samāgamma unnādino uccāsaddamahāsaddā anekavihitaṁ tiracchānakathaṁ kathentā nisinnā honti.
root_text.an10.94:3.1: Addasaṁsu kho te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā vajjiyamāhitaṁ gahapatiṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ. Disvāna aññamaññaṁ saṇṭhāpesuṁ:
root_text.an10.94:3.3: Ayaṁ vajjiyamāhito gahapati āgacchati samaṇassa gotamassa sāvako.
root_text.an10.94:3.4: Yāvatā kho pana samaṇassa gotamassa sāvakā gihī odātavasanā campāyaṁ paṭivasanti, ayaṁ tesaṁ aññataro vajjiyamāhito gahapati.
root_text.an10.94:3.5: Appasaddakāmā kho pana te āyasmanto appasaddavinītā appasaddassa vaṇṇavādino.
root_text.an10.94:3.6: Appeva nāma appasaddaṁ parisaṁ viditvā upasaṅkamitabbaṁ maññeyyā”ti.
root_text.an10.94:4.1: Atha kho te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā tuṇhī ahesuṁ.
root_text.an10.94:4.2: Atha kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati yena te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.94:4.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho vajjiyamāhitaṁ gahapatiṁ te aññatitthiyā paribbājakā etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an10.94:4.4: “saccaṁ kira, gahapati, samaṇo gotamo sabbaṁ tapaṁ garahati, sabbaṁ tapassiṁ lūkhājīviṁ ekaṁsena upakkosati upavadatī”ti?
root_text.an10.94:4.5: “Na kho, bhante, bhagavā sabbaṁ tapaṁ garahati napi sabbaṁ tapassiṁ lūkhājīviṁ ekaṁsena upakkosati upavadati.
root_text.an10.94:4.6: Gārayhaṁ kho, bhante, bhagavā garahati, pasaṁsitabbaṁ pasaṁsati.
root_text.an10.94:4.7: Gārayhaṁ kho pana, bhante, bhagavā garahanto pasaṁsitabbaṁ pasaṁsanto vibhajjavādo bhagavā.
root_text.an10.94:5.1: Evaṁ vutte, aññataro paribbājako vajjiyamāhitaṁ gahapatiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.94:5.2: “āgamehi tvaṁ, gahapati, yassa tvaṁ samaṇassa gotamassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsati, samaṇo gotamo venayiko appaññattiko”ti?
root_text.an10.94:5.6: Iti kusalākusalaṁ bhagavā paññāpayamāno sapaññattiko bhagavā;
root_text.an10.94:6.1: Evaṁ vutte te paribbājakā tuṇhībhūtā maṅkubhūtā pattakkhandhā adhomukhā pajjhāyantā appaṭibhānā nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.an10.94:6.2: Atha kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati te paribbājake tuṇhībhūte maṅkubhūte pattakkhandhe adhomukhe pajjhāyante appaṭibhāne viditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.an10.94:6.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati yāvatako ahosi tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ kathāsallāpo taṁ sabbaṁ bhagavato ārocesi.
root_text.an10.94:7.2: Evaṁ kho te, gahapati, moghapurisā kālena kālaṁ sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggahetabbā.
root_text.an10.94:7.3: Nāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ tapaṁ tapitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.4: na ca panāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ tapaṁ na tapitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.5: nāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ samādānaṁ samāditabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.6: na panāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ samādānaṁ na samāditabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.7: nāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ padhānaṁ padahitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.8: na panāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbaṁ padhānaṁ na padahitabbanti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.9: nāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbo paṭinissaggo paṭinissajjitabboti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:7.10: Na panāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbo paṭinissaggo na paṭinissajjitabboti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.11: nāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbā vimutti vimuccitabbāti vadāmi;
root_text.an10.94:7.12: na panāhaṁ, gahapati, sabbā vimutti na vimuccitabbāti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:8.1: Yañhi, gahapati, tapaṁ tapato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ tapaṁ na tapitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:8.2: Yañca khvassa, gahapati, tapaṁ tapato akusalā dhammā parihāyanti, kusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ tapaṁ tapitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:9.1: Yañhi, gahapati, samādānaṁ samādiyato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ samādānaṁ na samāditabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:9.2: Yañca khvassa, gahapati, samādānaṁ samādiyato akusalā dhammā parihāyanti, kusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ samādānaṁ samāditabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:10.1: Yañhi, gahapati, padhānaṁ padahato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpaṁ padhānaṁ na padahitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:10.2: Yañca khvassa, gahapati, padhānaṁ padahato akusalā dhammā parihāyanti kusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpaṁ padhānaṁ padahitabbanti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:11.1: Yañhi, gahapati, paṭinissaggaṁ paṭinissajjato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpo paṭinissaggo na paṭinissajjitabboti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:11.2: Yañca khvassa, gahapati, paṭinissaggaṁ paṭinissajjato akusalā dhammā parihāyanti, kusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpo paṭinissaggo paṭinissajjitabboti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:12.1: Yañhi, gahapati, vimuttiṁ vimuccato akusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, kusalā dhammā parihāyanti, evarūpā vimutti na vimuccitabbāti vadāmi.
root_text.an10.94:12.2: Yañca khvassa, gahapati, vimuttiṁ vimuccato akusalā dhammā parihāyanti, kusalā dhammā abhivaḍḍhanti, evarūpā vimutti vimuccitabbāti vadāmī”ti.
root_text.an10.94:13.1: Atha kho vajjiyamāhito gahapati bhagavatā dhammiyā kathāya sandassito samādapito samuttejito sampahaṁsito uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkāmi.
root_text.an10.94:14.1: Atha kho bhagavā acirapakkante vajjiyamāhite gahapatimhi bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.94:14.2: “yopi so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dīgharattaṁ apparajakkho imasmiṁ dhammavinaye, sopi evamevaṁ aññatitthiye paribbājake sahadhammena suniggahitaṁ niggaṇheyya yathā taṁ vajjiyamāhitena gahapatinā niggahitā”ti.

translation_text.an10.94:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Campā on the banks of the Gaggarā Lotus Pond.
translation_text.an10.94:1.2: Then the householder Vajjiyamāhita left Sāvatthī in the middle of the day to see the Buddha.
translation_text.an10.94:1.4: “It’s the wrong time to see the Buddha,
translation_text.an10.94:1.6: And it’s the wrong time to see the esteemed mendicants,
translation_text.an10.94:1.8: Why don’t I visit the monastery of the wanderers of other religions?”
translation_text.an10.94:2.1: Then he went to the monastery of the wanderers of other religions.
translation_text.an10.94:2.2: Now at that time, the wanderers of other religions had come together, making an uproar, a colossal racket as they sat and talked about all kinds of low topics.
translation_text.an10.94:3.1: They saw Vajjiyamāhita coming off in the distance, and stopped each other, saying,
translation_text.an10.94:3.2: “Be quiet, good fellows, don’t make a sound.
translation_text.an10.94:3.3: The householder Vajjiyamāhita, a disciple of the ascetic Gotama, is coming into our monastery.
translation_text.an10.94:3.4: He is included among the white-clothed lay disciples of the ascetic Gotama, who is residing near Campā.
translation_text.an10.94:3.5: Such venerables like the quiet, are educated to be quiet, and praise the quiet.
translation_text.an10.94:3.6: Hopefully if he sees that our assembly is quiet he’ll see fit to approach.”
translation_text.an10.94:4.1: Then those wanderers of other religions fell silent.
translation_text.an10.94:4.2: Then Vajjiyamāhita went up to them, and exchanged greetings with the wanderers there.
translation_text.an10.94:4.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. The wanderers said to him:
translation_text.an10.94:4.4: “Is it really true, householder? Does the ascetic Gotama criticize all forms of mortification? Does he categorically condemn and denounce those fervent mortifiers who live rough?”
translation_text.an10.94:4.5: “No, sirs, the Buddha does not criticize all forms of mortification. Nor does he categorically condemn and denounce those fervent mortifiers who live rough.
translation_text.an10.94:4.6: The Buddha criticizes where it is due, and praises where it is due.
translation_text.an10.94:4.7: In doing so he is one who speaks after analyzing the question,
translation_text.an10.94:4.8: and is not one-sided on this point.”
translation_text.an10.94:5.1: When he said this, one of the wanderers said to him,
translation_text.an10.94:5.2: “Hold on, householder! That ascetic Gotama who you praise is an exterminator who refrains from making statements.”
translation_text.an10.94:5.3: “On this point, also, I reasonably respond to the venerables.
translation_text.an10.94:5.4: The Buddha has stated ‘This is skillful’ and
translation_text.an10.94:5.6: So when it comes to what is skillful and unskillful the Buddha makes a statement.
translation_text.an10.94:5.7: He is not an exterminator who refrains from making statements.”
translation_text.an10.94:6.1: When this was said, those wanderers sat silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, downcast, depressed, with nothing to say.
translation_text.an10.94:6.2: Seeing this, Vajjiyamāhita got up from his seat. He went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side,
translation_text.an10.94:6.3: and informed the Buddha of all they had discussed.
translation_text.an10.94:7.2: That’s how you should legitimately and completely refute those futile men from time to time.
translation_text.an10.94:7.3: Householder, I don’t say that all mortifications should be undergone.
translation_text.an10.94:7.4: But I don’t say that no mortifications should be undergone.
translation_text.an10.94:7.5: I don’t say that all observances should be undertaken.
translation_text.an10.94:7.6: But I don’t say that no observances should be undertaken.
translation_text.an10.94:7.7: I don’t say that all efforts should be tried.
translation_text.an10.94:7.8: But I don’t say that no efforts should be tried.
translation_text.an10.94:7.9: I don’t say that everything should be given up.
translation_text.an10.94:7.10: But I don’t say that nothing should be given up.
translation_text.an10.94:7.11: I don’t say that you should be liberated with all kinds of freedom.
translation_text.an10.94:7.12: But I don’t say that you should not be liberated with any kind of freedom.
translation_text.an10.94:8.1: When undergoing certain mortifications, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t undergo those mortifications.
translation_text.an10.94:8.2: When undergoing certain mortifications, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should undergo those mortifications.
translation_text.an10.94:9.1: When undertaking certain observances, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t undertake those observances.
translation_text.an10.94:9.2: When undertaking certain observances, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should undertake those observances.
translation_text.an10.94:10.1: When trying certain efforts, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t try those efforts.
translation_text.an10.94:10.2: When trying certain efforts, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should try those efforts.
translation_text.an10.94:11.1: When giving up certain things, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t give up those things.
translation_text.an10.94:11.2: When giving up certain things, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should give up those things.
translation_text.an10.94:12.1: When being liberated with certain kinds of freedom, unskillful qualities grow while skillful qualities decline. I say that you shouldn’t be liberated with those kinds of freedom.
translation_text.an10.94:12.2: When being liberated with certain kinds of freedom, unskillful qualities decline while skillful qualities grow. I say that you should be liberated with those kinds of freedom.”
translation_text.an10.94:13.1: After Vajjiyamāhita had been educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired with a Dhamma talk by the Buddha, he got up from his seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha before leaving.
translation_text.an10.94:14.1: Then, not long after Vajjiyamāhita had left, the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an10.94:14.2: “Mendicants, even a mendicant who for a long time has had little dust in their eye in this teaching and training would legitimately and completely refute those wanderers of other religions just as the householder Vajjiyamāhita did.”
